## APRENDIZADOS — UNIQUE MECHANISM WORKSHOP (99 novos elementos)

### Core Unique Mechanism Philosophy

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | We NEVER present a commoditized mechanism — only unique mechanisms | L1 | FIL-TB-069 | UMW |
| 2 | All roads lead to you — UM ensures Google searches lead back to YOUR offer | L1 | FIL-TB-070 | UMW |
| 3 | Different is better than better for attention; better produces conversion | L1 | FIL-TB-071 | UMW |
| 4 | Education is the new selling — we don't sell, we educate on mechanism | L1 | FIL-TB-072 | UMW |
| 5 | Hope is the most powerful motivator — UM re-introduces hope after failures | L1 | FIL-TB-073 | UMW |
| 6 | People don't buy products, they buy mechanisms — the HOW | L1 | FIL-TB-074 | UMW |
| 7 | "Unique" applies to PROSPECTS, not competitors | L1 | FIL-TB-075 | UMW |
| 8 | Transubstantiation: ordinary → extraordinary through naming (Bill Bonner/Agora) | L1 | FIL-TB-076 | UMW |

### Mechanism Classification & Identification

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | 4 Types: Existing > Unspoken > Transubstantiated > Metaphorical (priority order) | L2 | MM-TB-083 | UMW |
| 2 | Schlitz Beer model: competitors do it but don't talk about it → talk first | L2 | MM-TB-084 | UMW |
| 3 | Promise Exposure Spectrum: 4 levels of market sophistication | L2 | MM-TB-079 | UMW |
| 4 | Mental Opt-Out: commodity terms trigger "heard it, seen it" → use metaphorical | L2 | MM-TB-085 | UMW |
| 5 | Product vs Mechanism distinction: P90X = product, Muscle Confusion = mechanism | L2 | MM-TB-086 | UMW |
| 6 | Faux Mechanism: name without substance — ALWAYS interrogate BEFORE naming | L2 | MM-TB-089 | UMW |
| 7 | Diagnostic process IS your mechanism when methods vary by client | L2 | MM-TB-090 | UMW |

### Campaign Architecture & Thesis

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Campaign Thesis = Buying Belief: "The [SUPERIOR] way to [PROMISE] is with [UM]" | L2 | MM-TB-091 | UMW |
| 2 | 2 Argument Models: Root Cause Solution vs Better Solution | L2 | MM-TB-092 | UMW |
| 3 | 5 Core Campaign Chunks: BMI → Lead → Thesis → Argument → S.I.N. Offer | L2 | MM-TB-093 | UMW |
| 4 | Mechanism Interrogation Framework: 13 systematic questions | L4 | FW-TB-098 | UMW |
| 5 | Choose ONE argument model per campaign — not both | L3 | HEUR-TB-134 | UMW |
| 6 | Never lead with product — always lead with mechanism first | L3 | HEUR-TB-135 | UMW |

### Naming Strategy

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Avoid benefit-driven names; use credible/scientific/formal names | L3 | HEUR-TB-130 | UMW |
| 2 | Name should sound like conference/news program, not infomercial | L3 | HEUR-TB-131 | UMW |
| 3 | 16 naming vocabulary words: System, Framework, Method, Protocol, etc. | L3 | HEUR-TB-145 | UMW |
| 4 | 2-Step Naming: Interrogate first (13Q), THEN name from findings | L5 | MET-TB-063 | UMW |

### Celebrity Positioning (10 Maven Secrets)

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Celebrity > Authority — more revenue, influence, longevity | L1 | FIL-TB-082 | UMW |
| 2 | Engineered celebrity is intentional, not organic — start the wave | L1 | FIL-TB-083 | UMW |
| 3 | Self-Aggrandizement (Gary Halbert): be your own biggest cheerleader | L2 | MM-TB-095 | UMW |
| 4 | Flag-in-the-Ground: own a topic in market's mind, find gaps | L2 | MM-TB-096 | UMW |
| 5 | 10 Maven Secrets framework for marketplace positioning | L4 | FW-TB-105 | UMW |
| 6 | Celebrity Engineering: 10-step continuous methodology | L5 | MET-TB-067 | UMW |

### Key Case Studies

| # | Case | Result | Mechanism |
|---|------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | P90X / Beach Body | $1B+ company | "Muscle Confusion" — tried 23 infomercials before finding it |
| 2 | Mass Control / Frank Kern | $5M+ in sales | Transubstantiated mechanism for email marketing |
| 3 | Schlitz Beer | Market leader | Unspoken mechanism (sterilized bottles — all brewers did it) |
| 4 | "Secret Currency" / Agora | Massive control | Metaphorical mechanism for gold investing |

## APRENDIZADOS — COPY LEGENDS LOCK-IN RECORDINGS (52 novos elementos)

### Research Methods (Collective Wisdom from 12+ Copy Legends)

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Research is about finding what's UNIQUE — fight "Curse of Sameness" (Kim Schwalm) | L1 | FIL-TB-059 | CLLR |
| 2 | Spy tools research: study top performers, reverse-engineer winners, create Flow Bible (Peter Kell) | L3 | HEUR-TB-103 | CLLR |
| 3 | "What almost made you not buy?" — thank you page survey that resurrects dying VSLs (Matt Stafford/Rich/Donnie) | L3 | HEUR-TB-104 | CLLR |
| 4 | AI survey analysis: 9-word email + ChatGPT → 38 content topics (Kevin Rogers) | L3 | HEUR-TB-112 | CLLR |
| 5 | Field testing: pitch under competitor brand at public venues (Donnie French) | L5 | MET-TB-059 | CLLR |
| 6 | Deep research for unique proof — full tech studies, not just abstracts (Kim Schwalm) | L5 | MET-TB-061 | CLLR |

### Big Ideas & Hooks

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Idea Hierarchy: Gimmicks → Promises → Notions → Ideas → Big Ideas (Stefan/Mark Ford) | L2 | MM-TB-068 | CLLR |
| 2 | "In or Out" Big Idea approach (David Deutsch) | L2 | MM-TB-069 | CLLR |
| 3 | Big Ideas have lifespans — what worked last year may not work today (Dan Ferrari) | L1 | FIL-TB-062 | CLLR |
| 4 | Big Ideas must stand without hyperbolic language (David Deutsch) | L2 | MM-TB-075 | CLLR |
| 5 | Most copy is playing too small — ideas, promises, gimmicks not big enough (Rob Martel) | L1 | FIL-TB-068 | CLLR |
| 6 | Winning hook recycling: same hook + new mechanism = new promo (Justin Goff) | L4 | FW-TB-095 | CLLR |

### Unique Mechanism & Believability

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Root cause → UM development: reframe problem, get buy-in, kindergarten simplicity (Peter/Todd) | L4 | FW-TB-091 | CLLR |
| 2 | Two-word mechanism naming: "Leptin Resistance", "Muscle Confusion" (Jon Benson) | L4 | FW-TB-092 | CLLR |
| 3 | UM campaigns more evergreen than Big Idea campaigns (Donnie/Dan) | L3 | HEUR-TB-114 | CLLR |
| 4 | Dream Life + Believability — need BOTH, one without other is loser (Jon Benson) | L3 | HEUR-TB-101 | CLLR |
| 5 | Compress promise for believability if needed — believable smaller > unbelievable bigger (Peter) | L1 | FIL-TB-067 | CLLR |
| 6 | Over-teasing mechanism name → replace with caveman language, numbers go UP (Donnie) | L3 | HEUR-TB-107 | CLLR |

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|

| 2 | How you bring customers in = how they behave — no discounting (Justin Goff) | L1 | FIL-TB-063 | CLLR |
| 3 | Dynamic upsell pricing: higher front-end = higher upsell (Donnie) | L3 | HEUR-TB-115 | CLLR |
| 4 | Front-end should contain absolute best stuff — wow for ascension (Rich Schefren) | L1 | FIL-TB-064 | CLLR |

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|

| 2 | VSL-hybrid: headline + subheadline BELOW VSL — Hot Jar tested (Donnie) | L3 | HEUR-TB-109 | CLLR |

## APRENDIZADOS — A-Z COPYWRITING WORKSHOP (105 novos elementos)

### Copywriting Craft & Writing

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Good copy is verb-driven, not adjective-driven. Verbs = movement, action, transformation. | L1 | FIL-TB-040 | AZCW |
| 2 | Copy must sound like spoken word. Simple/Short/Short. Flesch-Kincaid < 8. | L1 | FIL-TB-041 | AZCW |
| 3 | Replace adverbs with single strong verbs: slowly walk → strut, gently touch → caress | L3 | HEUR-TB-062 | AZCW |
| 4 | Use emotionally-charged words: embarrass → humiliate, tired → exhausted, broke → crushed | L3 | HEUR-TB-067 | AZCW |
| 5 | Don't emote, evoke! "You'll be excited when you hear" not "I'm so excited to tell you" | L1 | FIL-TB-044 | AZCW |
| 6 | Replace "if" with "when" (presupposition). "But" negates prior statement — use "and" instead. | L3 | HEUR-TB-064,066 | AZCW |

### Hooks, Themes & Ideas

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Categorical Imperative (Bonner/Agora): prevent prospect from categorizing idea into known bucket | L2 | MM-TB-045 | AZCW |
| 2 | Hook Rule of One (Masterson/Ford): one narrative, one promise, one emotion, one thought | L4 | FW-TB-055 | AZCW |
| 3 | Promise + Curiosity = foundation of all effective marketing hooks | L4 | FW-TB-057 | AZCW |
| 4 | Hook = emotionally compelling + intellectually interesting. Missing either = weak hook | L2 | MM-TB-056 | AZCW |
| 5 | "We are in the idea business" — Mark Ford, foundation of Agora's success | L1 | FIL-TB-048 | AZCW |
| 6 | A known tactic is a blown tactic. Tactics must feel invisible. | L1 | FIL-TB-042 | AZCW |

### Language Patterns & Persuasion

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | 15+ language pattern templates (presuppositional, excitement, future pacing, rapport, etc.) | L4 | FW-TB-068 | AZCW |
| 2 | Proof Power of 3: one = event, two = coincidence, three = pattern. Vary proof types. | L3 | HEUR-TB-069 | AZCW |
| 3 | Frame Control: same facts, different frame = different emotional response. | L2 | MM-TB-059 | AZCW |
| 4 | Zeigarnik Effect: open loops create mental tension that keeps prospects engaged. | L2 | MM-TB-058 | AZCW |
| 5 | Campaign Thesis formula: "The [superiority] way to [promise] is with [mechanism]." | L4 | FW-TB-063 | AZCW |
| 6 | Buying Belief: everything in body copy exists to establish this belief before the offer. | L4 | FW-TB-080 | AZCW |

## APRENDIZADOS POR DOMÍNIO

### Marketing Funnels (Core)

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |

| 2 | Front-end exists ONLY for customer acquisition, not profit | L1 | FIL-TB-001 | SFFF-M02 |
| 3 | Transaction Maximization Sequence has 4 offers: Reverse Add-On → Soft Add-On → Mid-Tier → Thank You Gift | L4 | FW-TB-022 | SFFF-M06 |
| 4 | Amplification Autoresponder Chain has 8 behavior-triggered sequences | L4 | FW-TB-024 | SFFF-M07 |
| 5 | The marketer who can spend the most to acquire a customer wins | L1 | FIL-TB-013 | SFFF-M07 |

### Offer Creation

|---|---------|-------|----|--------|

## PADRÕES DECISÓRIOS

| # | Padrão | Regra | Source |
|---|--------|-------|--------|
| 1 | Pricing front-end offers | Always $19-$29 for CCO — low barrier, high quality filter | HEUR-TB-002 |
| 2 | Campaign viability | Compare EPC vs CPC first; if EPC > CPC, campaign is viable | HEUR-TB-037 |
| 3 | When to kill a campaign | Only when CPS > AFEV AND no proven back-end | HEUR-TB-039 |
| 4 | What to test first | Offer changes first (can double response), then headline, then big idea | HEUR-TB-041 |
| 5 | Upsell pricing | Always fraction of original: Reverse ≤80%, Soft $67-97, Mid $297-497 | HEUR-TB-016,018,021 |
| 6 | Niche validation | Must pass 6-criteria mob test + verify competitors are advertising | HEUR-TB-001,004 |
| 7 | Break-even response | Keep running — break-even acquisition = free customers for back-end | HEUR-TB-036 |
| 8 | Stagnant LCV | Diagnose as insufficient offer frequency → create more products | HEUR-TB-038 |

## APRENDIZADOS — YOUR WINNING OFFER (73 novos elementos)

### Core YWO Philosophy

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | Marketing is the art of meaningful differentiation | L1 | FIL-TB-094 | YWO |
| 2 | Differentiation is not discretionary (Levitt) — it's mandatory | L1 | FIL-TB-095 | YWO |
| 3 | Just having a funnel is no longer enough — you need a winning offer | L1 | FIL-TB-096 | YWO |
| 4 | An offer is not a product plus a bonus — it's a SIN Proposition | L1 | FIL-TB-097 | YWO |
| 5 | Examine competitors to know what NOT to say | L1 | FIL-TB-098 | YWO |
| 6 | Go the distance with naming and framing of deliverables | L1 | FIL-TB-099 | YWO |
| 7 | The winning offer is your single most valuable asset | L1 | FIL-TB-103 | YWO |
| 8 | Funnel hacking is the wrong approach to competition | L1 | FIL-TB-107 | YWO |

### SIN Proposition & Attractive Panacea

| # | Insight | Layer | ID | Source |
|---|---------|-------|----|--------|
| 1 | SIN = Superior + Irresistible + Noteworthy — the acid test for any offer | L1 | FIL-TB-110 | YWO |
| 2 | Attractive Panacea = prospect's dream solution — align your offer with it | L1 | FIL-TB-108 | YWO |
| 3 | SIN Proposition Model: three-part framework | L2 | MM-TB-101 | YWO |
| 4 | Attractive Panacea Model: ideal solution as perceived by prospect | L2 | MM-TB-102 | YWO |
| 5 | SIN Proposition Construction Framework: 5-step build process | L4 | FW-TB-113 | YWO |
| 6 | SIN test before every launch | L3 | HEUR-TB-155 | YWO |
